title: Learn OOF pit-window hazard priors instead of fixed tyre-age windows
summary: This target is naturally a discrete-time event hazard: the probability of stopping on the next lap given the driver has not yet stopped this lap.
rationale: This target is naturally a discrete-time event hazard: the probability of stopping on the next lap given the driver has not yet stopped this lap. Discrete-time survival literature fits this setup well, and it is a cleaner generalization of the current hard-coded pit-window heuristics.
implementation_hint: Build smoothed out-of-fold lookup features such as logit P(PitNextLap=1 | Compound, TyreLife bucket, Stint, RaceProgress bucket, wet/dry, Race or Year). Feed these priors into the existing tabular model, or test a simple complementary-log-log / logistic meta-model on top of them.
expected_effect: Should outperform manual window constants by learning track- and compound-specific stopping curves while staying simple enough for tabular boosters.
risk: Sparse combinations can overfit; use hierarchical backoff and additive smoothing.
